title: Samsung, SK Hynix and Micron sued for alleged DRAM memory price fixing
summary: Chipmakers are accused of coordinating the reduction of DDR3 and DDR4 production to raise prices, in a class action lawsuit filed in the United States.

title: WhatsApp introduces usernames to protect phone number privacy
summary: The messaging platform allows reserving a unique username, avoiding sharing the personal number, and the feature is now available to some users.

title: South Korea announces multi-million dollar investment in memory chip manufacturing and artificial intelligence
summary: The Asian country and its main semiconductor manufacturers commit more than $500 billion to build new factories and HBM centers, facing growing demand for AI.

title: SteamOS comes to desktop PCs with the first preinstalled units and new Steam Machines
summary: Valve promotes its gaming operating system for desktop computers, with models like the Steamroller and the arrival of more powerful and affordable custom Steam Machines.

title: Nvidia GeForce RTX 3060 goes back on sale in Europe five years after its launch
summary: The 12 GB graphics card reappears in the retail market at a price of around 333 euros, confirming its resurgence after being considered a good idea by Jensen Huang.

title: Dbrand withdraws its Companion Cube case for Steam Machine after Valve's legal threat
summary: The accessory manufacturer canceled the product inspired by Portal 2 after Valve requested a license and admitted it had no right to manufacture it, offering refunds.

title: Anthropic signs deals with California and the EU to expand use of its Claude model
summary: The AI company offers its assistant to the California administration at half price, while the EU seeks to attract it to Europe with investments, and restores the Mythos 5 version for critical infrastructure.

title: AMD launches new Adrenalin driver, reviews FSR 4.1 and warns about DXVK 3.0 on Windows
summary: The company updates its graphics suite with version 26.6.4, FSR 4.1 reviews for RDNA 3 appear, and performance issues on Windows 10 are reported in addition to a warning about not using DXVK 3.0 on RDNA1 and RDNA2.

title: Tencent signs a $2.94 billion agreement with Chinese CXMT for DRAM memory supply
summary: In the midst of the semiconductor trade war, the Chinese tech giant secures memory chips with the domestic manufacturer, strengthening self-sufficiency.

title: Rocket Lab buys satellite company Iridium and continues its space expansion
summary: The launch company acquires the satellite constellation, strengthening its presence in the communications and orbital services sector.

title: U.S. Supreme Court rules that geofence warrants require constitutional protection
summary: The high court rules that police need a warrant with probable cause to access bulk location data, a win for privacy.

title: Digi confirms its stock market listing to raise funds and expand its fiber and mobile network
summary: The Spanish telecommunications operator announces its initial public offering, aiming to raise capital to continue deploying infrastructure, supported by its relationship with Mayoral.

title: CachyOS adds support for Hyprland Noctalia and performance improvements in Python and GCC
summary: The gaming-optimized Linux distribution incorporates new features such as Extended PGO, DNS-over-QUIC and a Noctalia profile for Hyprland.

title: Google expands AI image generation with Gemini for users in the US and rations access to Meta
summary: Gemini offers customized image creation for free in the US, while the shortage of computing capacity forces Google to limit access to Meta and other customers.

title: DDR5 RAM prices drop rapidly and an EXPO ULL kit reaches $1,099
summary: A tracking of the European market shows an accelerated drop in DDR5 prices, while the AMD EXPO ULL DDR5-6000 C26 kit is sold at an 80% premium.

title: Nintendo Switch 2 price increases in South Korea and estimates for the PS6 price emerge
summary: Nintendo's console experiences a 17% increase in September, while analysts predict that the future PlayStation 6 could cost more, in a context of general price increases in Spain.

title: The Vivo X Fold6 prepares to arrive in Spain while the release date of the iPhone 18 Pro is leaked
summary: Vivo's most ambitious foldable targets Europe, and possible release dates for the upcoming iPhone 18 Pro, Pro Max and Ultra are known.

title: Trump threatens 100% tariffs on Europe and his administration puts obstacles to 92 GW of new electricity
summary: The US president warns of trade sanctions if the EU regulates tech companies, while bureaucracy slows down the development of electrical infrastructure.

title: Meta disguised contractors as teenagers to incite rival chatbots about suicide and drugs, and the US Army bombed a school by AI error
summary: Reports reveal questionable practices by Meta against competitors and a failure in a military targeting system that ignored a note about a school.

title: The New York Times accuses Microsoft of helping OpenAI use its copyrighted content and Amazon distills Anthropic models to save costs
summary: The newspaper sues Microsoft for facilitating copyright infringement in AI training, while Amazon finds ways to reduce costs using Anthropic's models.
